title: 2 Boston men accused of assaulting man who hit their car while trying to park
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Two Boston men, Anthony Shearrion and Arkie Swinson, charged after allegedly assaulting a man who hit their car.
Incident occurred near Blue Hill Avenue and Abbot Street; victim suffered a laceration and was hospitalized.
Shearrion and Swinson held at Suffolk County Jail; court date set for April 14 after pleading not guilty.

title: 2026 Red Sox preview: 4 questions facing Alex Cora’s club
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The 2026 Red Sox face key questions regarding their lineup and bullpen effectiveness under manager Alex Cora.
After a solid 2025, expectations are high, boosted by talents like Roman Anthony and improvements in rotation strength.
Infield defense and an outfield/DH logjam pose significant challenges despite new personnel such as Willson Contreras.

title: Healey announces kickoff of public safety initiative for 2026 World Cup
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey announced Massachusetts' public safety plan for the 2026 World Cup, with seven matches at Gillette Stadium.
Healey secured $76 million in federal funds for security, focusing on cybersecurity, emergency preparedness, and public health.
MassDOT will implement special transportation measures, including 'event trains' and adjusted MBTA schedules for summer matches.

title: Kraft-owned New England Revolution backing White Stadium renovation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The Kraft-owned New England Revolution supports the renovation of White Stadium in Franklin Park, Boston.
The renovation, funded through a public-private partnership, has faced legal opposition citing misuse of public parkland.
Boston Legacy FC will invest $190 million, while taxpayers will contribute $135 million, amid promises of community benefits.

title: Maine man in critical condition after stopping to help stranded driver
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 54-year-old Maine man is critically injured after being struck by a car while helping a stranded driver on I-95 northbound.
The man and an 18-year-old woman were hit by a car driven by a 19-year-old woman who lost control in snowy conditions.
The man may have tried to shield the woman during the crash; the incident remains under investigation by police.

title: Berkshire County man arrested after suspicious early-morning fire in his home
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Rohane, 60, of Monterey, was arrested following a suspicious fire at his home on Main Road.
No injuries were reported from the fire, but it was determined to have been intentionally set.
Rohane was found in a wooded area and taken to a hospital for reasons unrelated to the fire or arrest.

title: Lured by pepperoni, dog rescued from 12-foot backyard sinkhole on Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An 11-year-old Labrador named Tessa was rescued from a 12-foot sinkhole in Falmouth using pepperoni.
Falmouth Fire and Rescue crews responded to a yard incident suspected to be caused by an underground pipe leak.
Rescuers used a pole leash to pull Tessa out uninjured, after failed attempts with a gurney system.

title: New England-based rescue takes in batch of neglected axolotls
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Libertyland Axolotl Rescue took in 16 neglected axolotls from an overcrowding situation, addressing issues like missing limbs and infections.
The rescue, established in July 2024, is the only US nonprofit focused solely on axolotls, with a network of foster parents.
Donations are sought for veterinary care and supplies as the axolotls, named after cheeses, undergo rehabilitation and regrow limbs.

title: The beauty of a few cracks
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: A24 offers Love Letters tickets to a screening of 'The Drama' in Somerville, starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson.
Meredith Goldstein shares a personal story of friendship marked by damage on a kitchen doorframe as a symbol of love.
Podcast guest Yowei Shaw recounts a burnt table incident, a lesson in personal growth and relationship dynamics.

title: Waltham High opens student-run salon for real-world cosmetology training
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Waltham High School opens a 25-station student-run salon for hands-on cosmetology training.
Students gain experience in hair, skin, and nail care, working toward state licensure with 1,000 training hours.
The school salon offers services to the public under instructor supervision, appointments required via email or phone.
